Its platform engineers fungi as industrial biofactories to produce stable, vibrant natural colors, flavors and antioxidant ingredients; Red+ is positioned as a pH-stable, heat-resistant natural red colorant for multiple food categories. It belongs inside BIO VC LATAM as precision-fermented food ingredients and biomanufacturing infrastructure replacing petroleum- or crop-derived colorants.
business_one_liner: Fungal precision-fermentation platform for natural ingredients.
problem_addressed: Synthetic and crop-derived food colorants face health, performance, land-use, water-use, supply-chain and sustainability constraints.
technical_approach: Fungal biofactories, bioinformatics, synthetic biology, precision fermentation and scale-up optimization for natural colors, flavors and functional ingredients.
industry_destination: Food ingredients, colors, flavors and consumer products
thesis_scope_note: Include as core food biotech and bioindustrial ingredient production through fungal precision fermentation.
evidence_excerpt: Official site says Michroma creates natural ingredients with fungi, engineers high-performance industrial biofactories through bioinformatics and synthetic biology, and uses precision fermentation to produce scalable natural ingredients.

startup_summary_v1: Stämm is an Argentine advanced-biomanufacturing company developing high-throughput bioprocessors, bioengineering services and Bio AI tools for biologics and cell-therapy production. Its platform supports cell-line development, biologics manufacturing, cell-therapy workflows and multi-omic data analysis, with continuous bioreactor technology aimed at increasing antibody productivity. It belongs inside BIO VC LATAM as core biomanufacturing infrastructure for biologics, cell therapies and data-driven bioengineering.
business_one_liner: High-throughput bioprocessing and Bio AI for biologics manufacturing.
problem_addressed: Conventional biologics and cell-therapy manufacturing is slow, capacity-constrained and difficult to scale or optimize.
technical_approach: Continuous bioprocessing, high-throughput bioprocessors, bioengineering, cell-line development, multi-omics data analysis and generative AI biological models.
thesis_scope_note: Include as core biomanufacturing infrastructure and one of the strongest LATAM examples of advanced bioindustrial tooling.
evidence_excerpt: Official site describes a high-throughput bioprocessor, bioengineering solutions for cell-line development, Bio AI pipelines using multi-omics data, and workflows for biologics and cell therapies.

startup_summary_v1: Elytron Biotech is an Argentine bioindustrial company transforming frontier technology into high-performance biological products for agriculture and bioindustry. Its AI-driven platform is designed to accelerate development, reduce industrialization risk and integrate market-ready innovations into bioindustry value chains, with team capabilities in genomics, gene editing, molecular biology, bioinformatics and agricultural pest problems. It belongs inside BIO VC LATAM as biological-product development infrastructure for ag and bioindustrial systems.
business_one_liner: AI-driven biological product development platform.
problem_addressed: Biological products for agriculture and bioindustry are slow to develop, risky to industrialize and hard to integrate into value chains.
technical_approach: AI-driven product-development platform, genomics, gene editing, molecular biology, bioinformatics and industrialization workflows for biological products.
thesis_scope_note: Include as core bioindustrial platform; current source is company-level rather than product-specific, so maintain medium/high confidence until product pipeline is more explicit.
evidence_excerpt: Official site says Elytron transforms cutting-edge technology into high-performance biological products through an AI-driven platform that accelerates development, minimizes industrialization risk and integrates into bioindustry value chains.

startup_summary_v1: AVaTAR MedTech is an Argentine/US cardiovascular medtech company developing a surgical method and device set for aortic and pulmonary valve reconstruction using the patient's own autologous tissue. Its AVaTAR approach standardizes semilunar valve reconstruction for children and adults, aiming to avoid lifelong anticoagulation, support pediatric growth and make procedures more reproducible. It belongs inside BIO VC LATAM as high-quality medical-device and tissue-based cardiovascular repair infrastructure.
business_one_liner: Autologous tissue heart-valve reconstruction devices.
problem_addressed: Valvular heart disease and congenital valve defects require complex surgeries, prosthetic valves, anticoagulation or devices that may not accommodate pediatric growth.
technical_approach: Surgical method, additive-manufactured/injection-molded instruments, autologous pericardial tissue reconstruction and standardized valve-repair workflow.
industry_destination: Cardiovascular surgery and structural heart disease
thesis_scope_note: Include as medtech with tissue-based repair; not core biotech, but directly biological and clinically material.
evidence_excerpt: Official site describes AVaTAR as a surgical method and set of medical devices for reconstructing aortic or pulmonary valves in children and adults with autologous tissue.

startup_summary_v1: Argentine/LatAm online insurance marketplace focused on comparing and buying insurance products such as car, home, personal accident and related coverage. It is a credible venture-backed digital business, but current public evidence shows insurance distribution and brokerage rather than biology, biobased materials, biocentric production systems or planetary-boundary stewardship. It should remain outside BIO VC LATAM as a non-material fintech/insurtech adjacent to the broader VC ecosystem.
business_one_liner: Online insurance comparison and brokerage marketplace.
problem_addressed: Fragmented insurance discovery, quoting and purchasing.
technical_approach: Digital marketplace and quote flow for insurance products.
thesis_scope_note: Exclude: relevant to LATAM VC, but no material BIO, biological-system or planetary-boundary role in public evidence.
evidence_excerpt: Official website presents 123Seguro as an online insurance marketplace for comparing and buying insurance products.

startup_summary_v1: Brazilian fashion and lifestyle e-commerce brand selling apparel, basics, swimwear, jeans, accessories and related consumer products through a digital retail experience. It belongs in a broad LATAM VC landscape, but not in the BIO VC LATAM thesis: public evidence points to consumer fashion retail rather than biobased materials, circular textile innovation, biological manufacturing or planetary-boundary stewardship. It should stay excluded unless future evidence shows a specific biomaterials or regenerative textile line.
business_one_liner: Brazilian fashion and lifestyle e-commerce brand.
problem_addressed: Digital access and merchandising for consumer fashion.
technical_approach: Online storefront, product catalog, logistics and consumer-brand operations.
thesis_scope_note: Exclude: fashion commerce, not biomaterials or regenerative textile technology in current evidence.
evidence_excerpt: Official site shows AMARO as a fashion retail store with categories such as clothing, jeans, swimwear, basics and collections.

startup_summary_v1: Brazilian B2B payments infrastructure company offering omnichannel payments, custom checkout, receivables management, acquiring-as-a-service, corporate banking, fraud controls, reconciliation and financial dashboards for large companies and fintechs. It is well sourced and relevant to LATAM fintech, but current evidence shows horizontal financial infrastructure rather than BIO VC LATAM materiality. It remains excluded because it does not operate on biological systems, biobased products or planetary-boundary stewardship.
business_one_liner: B2B payments, acquiring and corporate banking infrastructure.
problem_addressed: Complex payment acceptance, reconciliation, fraud and treasury workflows.
technical_approach: Modular payments, acquiring-as-a-service and banking APIs/platform tools.
thesis_scope_note: Exclude: strong fintech company, but no BIO/material/planetary-boundary thesis fit.
evidence_excerpt: Official site describes Barte as payment infrastructure for large companies and fintechs, including omnichannel payments, acquiring-as-a-service and corporate banking.

startup_summary_v1: Brazilian fleet-management and telematics platform that helps companies track vehicles, monitor driver behavior, reduce fuel and maintenance costs, improve safety and manage fleet operations through video telemetry, CAN telemetry, mobile apps and analytics. It may support operational efficiency and lower emissions indirectly, but it is not specifically biobased, biocentric or a planetary-boundary stewardship company. It should stay excluded from BIO VC LATAM while remaining a useful example of adjacent climate-efficiency software.
business_one_liner: Fleet tracking, video telemetry and logistics-efficiency platform.
problem_addressed: Low visibility, safety risk and operational inefficiency in vehicle fleets.
technical_approach: Vehicle tracking, video telemetry, telemetry data and mobile workflows.
thesis_scope_note: Exclude: indirect efficiency signal is not enough for BIO thesis without biological/material/planetary-boundary specificity.
evidence_excerpt: Official site describes Cobli as a fleet platform with video telemetry, telemetry and mobile apps to reduce costs and improve productivity and safety.

startup_summary_v1: Tissue Dynamics is a therapeutics and drug-discovery company using AI, human organoids, robotic automation and real-time sensor platforms to generate human-relevant safety, efficacy and patient-response data. It is clearly biotech and would fit a global bio map, but it should remain outside BIO VC LATAM because the company publicly lists locations in France and Israel, not a LATAM base or founding signal in our current evidence. This is a high-quality exclude, not a weak digital stub.
business_one_liner: Human organoid and AI platform for drug discovery and toxicology.
problem_addressed: Poor translation from traditional preclinical models to human drug response.
technical_approach: Human organoids, robotics, sensors and AI software for drug discovery and toxicology.
thesis_scope_note: Exclude only on geography, not on bio relevance: strong biotech but no LATAM base in current evidence.
evidence_excerpt: Official site describes real-time organ physiology, AI platforms, human organoids, robotic automation and lists locations in France and Israel.

startup_summary_v1: Argentine materials-science startup developing antimicrobial nanotechnology and bioactive surface/material applications. The current evidence is not yet as deep as a full company technical profile, but it is materially closer to the BIO VC LATAM thesis than to generic software: its value proposition is a physical antimicrobial technology for health, textiles or surface-material use cases. It should move from provisional exclude to include-under-review as a bioactive materials and advanced-materials case, with follow-up needed to confirm product maturity and exact biological mechanism.
business_one_liner: Antimicrobial nanotechnology for bioactive materials and surfaces.
problem_addressed: Microbial contamination and infection risk in surfaces, textiles and health-adjacent materials.
technical_approach: Nanotechnology-enabled antimicrobial material or coating applications.
thesis_scope_note: Promote from stub exclude to include-under-review because public signal is material and bioactive rather than generic digital.
evidence_excerpt: Public web evidence describes Hybridon as an Argentine antimicrobial nanotechnology/materials startup; needs deeper technical source follow-up.
